majors.notes: official Columbia admissions pages were blocked by CloudFront in this environment; Columbia bulletin pages were partially accessible but not enough to derive a reliable undergraduate majors total here; titles extracted from official page: https://bulletin.columbia.edu/azindex/

admissions.application_platform: Common Application, Coalition Application, or QuestBridge Application
admissions.testing_policy: Cross-verified from the Common App school page: Columbia lists optional SAT or ACT scores (self-reported) for first-year applicants.
admissions.gpa_policy: No explicit minimum GPA is stated on the Common App school profile used in this cross-verified repair pass; Columbia instead describes a holistic review process in which every part of the application matters.
admissions.recommendations: Cross-verified from the Common App school page: Columbia lists a secondary school report and two teacher recommendations for first-year applicants.
admissions.essays: Cross-verified from the Common App school page: Columbia lists Columbia-specific questions as part of the first-year application.

evidence.claim: Common Application, Coalition Application, or QuestBridge Application
evidence.source_excerpt: We ask first-year applicants to submit the Common Application, Coalition Application or QuestBridge Application with Columbia-specific questions, a secondary school report, two teacher recommendations and optional SAT or ACT scores (self-reported).

evidence.claim: Cross-verified from the Common App school page: Columbia lists optional SAT or ACT scores (self-reported) for first-year applicants.
evidence.source_excerpt: We ask first-year applicants to submit the Common Application, Coalition Application or QuestBridge Application with Columbia-specific questions, a secondary school report, two teacher recommendations and optional SAT or ACT scores (self-reported).

evidence.claim: Cross-verified from the Common App school page: Columbia lists a secondary school report and two teacher recommendations for first-year applicants.
evidence.source_excerpt: We ask first-year applicants to submit the Common Application, Coalition Application or QuestBridge Application with Columbia-specific questions, a secondary school report, two teacher recommendations and optional SAT or ACT scores (self-reported).

evidence.claim: Cross-verified from the Common App school page: Columbia lists Columbia-specific questions as part of the first-year application.
evidence.source_excerpt: We ask first-year applicants to submit the Common Application, Coalition Application or QuestBridge Application with Columbia-specific questions, a secondary school report, two teacher recommendations and optional SAT or ACT scores (self-reported).

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 professors from RateMyProfessors by rating count and sentiment metrics.
evidence.source_excerpt: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Columbia University' (legacyId=278) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 20 notable alumni from Wikipedia-derived candidate lists using trailing pageviews.
evidence.source_excerpt: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, and ranked by pageviews. Major-specific and last-10-years filtering requires stronger school-specific alumni sources than this generic pipeline currently has.

evidence.claim: No explicit minimum GPA is stated on the Common App school profile used in this cross-verified repair pass; Columbia instead describes a holistic review process in which every part of the application matters.
evidence.source_excerpt: We ask first-year applicants to submit the Common Application, Coalition Application or QuestBridge Application with Columbia-specific questions, a secondary school report, two teacher recommendations and optional SAT or ACT scores (self-reported). The admissions process at Columbia is a holistic one, which means that every part of the application matters to help inform our judgment.

warnings: Major titles extracted from official school source (26 titles).
Phase 4 structured upgrade applied to top-10 expansion schools.
Columbia admissions pages returned 403 / CloudFront blocks in this environment during phase 4.
Columbia bulletin pages were partially accessible, but a reliable structured admissions extraction was not completed in this pass.
Columbia admissions fields were cross-verified from the Common App school profile because official Columbia admissions pages were bot-protected in this environment.
School-people enrichment uses public third-party sources (RateMyProfessors and Wikipedia/Wikimedia), so rankings are heuristic rather than official university data.

popular_professors.ranking_basis: RateMyProfessors entries sorted by rating_count desc, then average_rating desc, then would_take_again_percent desc.
popular_professors.source_summary: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Columbia University' (legacyId=278) and ranked up to 20 professors with at least one rating.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Curated from official Columbia Magazine alumni features, prioritizing person-specific alumni stories with explicit school-year evidence and substantive professional or civic impact descriptions.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official Columbia Magazine alumni feature pages on magazine.columbia.edu.

items.name: Maria Weston Kuhn
items.bio: Columbia Magazine says Maria Weston Kuhn ’23CC founded Drive US Forward to push for more accurate female crash-test dummies and safer cars for women.
items.description: Road-safety advocate and founder of Drive US Forward.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Ryan Greene
items.bio: Columbia Magazine says Ryan Greene ’21BUS co-founded Quincy to help older adults navigate an increasingly digital world.
items.description: Tech-support startup co-founder focused on older adults.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Jackie Dubrovich
items.bio: Columbia Magazine says Jackie Dubrovich ’16CC finished the Olympics on top, earning gold after years as an elite foil fencer.
items.description: Olympic gold medal-winning fencer.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.

items.name: Suzanne Reisman

items.description: New York City tour guide and author.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official school or alumni-domain story/profile with explicit alumni identification and substantive career or impact details.
